attractions.name: Taylor Highway
attractions.season: Open roughly April/May to Oct 15; zero winter maintenance, gated shut in snow
attractions.blurb: 160-mi gravel/chip-seal road from Tetlin Junction over the Fortymile country to Chicken, Jack Wade and Eagle.
attractions.tip: Fuel only at Chicken and Eagle. Check 511.alaska.gov - washboard, frost heaves and no cell service most of it.

attractions.name: Chicken
attractions.season: Businesses open roughly mid-May to mid-Sept; near-deserted in winter
attractions.blurb: Population ~15 gold-camp settlement at Taylor Hwy MP 66: cafe, saloon, mercantile, outhouses and gift shops.
attractions.tip: Named Chicken because miners couldn't spell ptarmigan. Cash helps - card service drops out constantly.

attractions.name: Chicken Gold Camp & Pedro Dredge
attractions.season: Camp and dredge tours mid-May through mid-September
attractions.cost: Dredge tour ~$10-15; panning ~$20; RV/tent sites extra
attractions.blurb: Restored 1938 bucket-line dredge that worked Chicken Creek, moved here in 1998, plus a campground and cafe.
attractions.tip: Just off Taylor Hwy on Airport Road. Panning here uses real paydirt from Chicken Creek - bring your own pan to save.

attractions.name: Chickenstock Music Festival
attractions.season: One weekend each June, usually the second weekend, at Chicken Gold Camp
attractions.blurb: Two-day live-music weekend in a town of 15 people; Alaskan bands, camping in the gold-camp field.
attractions.tip: Book a campsite the moment dates post - Chicken's total lodging is a few dozen sites and it sells out.

attractions.name: West Fork Campground (BLM)
attractions.season: Open when the Taylor Highway is open, roughly May-September
attractions.blurb: 25-site BLM campground at Taylor Hwy MP 49 with an overlook onto an oxbow lake used by trumpeter swans and moose.
attractions.tip: Last easy camping before Chicken and the only water spigot for miles; pull-through sites fit big rigs.

attractions.name: Walker Fork Campground (BLM)
attractions.season: Open when the Taylor Highway is open, roughly May-September
attractions.blurb: 24-site BLM campground at Taylor Hwy MP 82 on the old Lassen airstrip, beside the Jack Wade mining creeks.
attractions.tip: Recreational hand-panning is allowed in the creek here - one of the few legal, unclaimed spots on the Taylor.

attractions.name: Fortymile Wild and Scenic River
attractions.season: Float season roughly late May to mid-September once ice is out
attractions.blurb: Roadside-accessible wild river system through the Fortymile gold district; multi-day canoe and raft floats.
attractions.tip: The Taylor crosses the South Fork at MP 75 with interpretive panels; most floats end at the Yukon in Canada.

attractions.name: Boundary
attractions.season: Reachable only when the Top of the World Highway is open, roughly June-September
attractions.blurb: Sod-roofed 1920s-30s roadhouse and airstrip three miles from the Canadian border; the lodge is closed.
attractions.tip: Nothing is for sale here anymore - fuel up in Chicken or Dawson, not Boundary. Photo stop only.

attractions.name: Poker Creek-Little Gold Creek Border Crossing
attractions.season: 2026 season roughly May 22-Sept 15, 8am-6pm Alaska time (9am-7pm Yukon); closed otherwise
attractions.blurb: Northernmost land border port in the US - one shared US/Canada building on a bare tundra ridge at 141 degrees W.
attractions.tip: Hours are short and strictly enforced; arrive by 5pm Alaska time. Passport required, no fuel, no cell service.

attractions.name: Top of the World Highway
attractions.season: Open roughly June to mid-September, tied to the border port and the Dawson ferry; no winter maintenance
attractions.blurb: 79-mi mostly gravel ridge road that runs along mountaintops above treeline from West Dawson to the Alaska border.
attractions.tip: No fuel for 108 mi between Dawson and Chicken. Fog on the ridge is common - drive it midday, not at dusk.

attractions.name: George Black Ferry
attractions.season: Runs 24 hrs a day mid-May to mid-October (weekly maintenance break); ice bridge replaces it in deep winter
attractions.blurb: Free Yukon government cable ferry across the Yukon River linking Dawson City to the Top of the World Highway.
attractions.tip: First-come queues at the foot of Front Street can run 1-2 hrs midday in July; tour buses get priority 6-9am and 5-9pm.

attractions.name: Dawson City
attractions.season: Year-round community; most tours, shows and Parks Canada sites run mid-May to mid-September
attractions.blurb: Klondike gold-rush town with dirt streets, boardwalks, Diamond Tooth Gerties gambling hall and Dredge No. 4.
attractions.tip: Passport needed and Yukon runs 1 hr ahead of Alaska - budget the time change when planning your border crossing.

attractions.name: Eagle
attractions.season: Road access April/May to mid-Oct only; museum tours run summer
attractions.blurb: Yukon River town of ~85 at the end of a 65-mi spur off the Taylor, with an intact 1900s courthouse and customs house.
attractions.tip: The Eagle Historical Society runs a 3-hr guided walking tour of five historic buildings, usually 9am daily in summer.
attractions.duration_note: half day plus long drive in

attractions.name: Fort Egbert & Eagle Historic District
attractions.season: Buildings open for tours in summer; grounds accessible whenever the Taylor Highway is open
attractions.blurb: Five BLM-restored buildings of an 1899 US Army post - the mule barn, quartermaster storehouse and granary survive.
attractions.tip: This is where Amundsen mushed 500 mi in 1905 to telegraph news that he'd completed the Northwest Passage.

attractions.name: Yukon-Charley Rivers National Preserve
attractions.season: River season roughly mid-June to mid-September once the Yukon is ice-free
attractions.cost: Free; charter boat/air from Eagle or Circle is the real cost
attractions.blurb: 2.5M-acre NPS preserve with no roads; a 158-mi Yukon River float from Eagle to Circle past cabins and bluffs.
attractions.tip: Slaven's Roadhouse and six other public-use cabins are free, first-come. Register your float at the Eagle NPS office.

attractions.name: Steese Highway
attractions.season: Maintained year-round to Circle, but Eagle and Twelvemile summits close in winter storms
attractions.blurb: 162-mi road from Fox over two alpine passes to Circle on the Yukon; pavement ends near Central.
attractions.tip: Fuel at Fox, Central and Circle only. Nothing is open after 8pm - fill up in Fairbanks before you commit.
attractions.duration_note: long day round trip from Fairbanks

attractions.name: Felix Pedro Monument
attractions.season: Roadside pullout accessible year-round
attractions.blurb: Stone marker at Steese MP 16.5 where Felice Pedroni struck gold on Pedro Creek in July 1902 and founded Fairbanks.
attractions.tip: Free panning is allowed in Pedro Creek across the road - it's the traditional Fairbanks Golden Days photo stop.

attractions.name: Cleary Summit
attractions.season: Aurora season late August to mid-April; the pullout is plowed all winter
attractions.blurb: 2,233-ft pass 20 mi up the Steese with a big north-facing pullout - the closest dark-sky aurora spot to Fairbanks.
attractions.tip: Above the Fairbanks valley inversion, so it is often clear and 20F warmer up here when town is fogged in.

attractions.name: Mt. Aurora Skiland
attractions.season: Ski area runs Dec-early April weekends; aurora viewing nights Aug-April
attractions.blurb: Small Cleary Summit ski hill whose lodge opens at night as a heated, ticketed aurora-viewing room.
attractions.tip: Cheaper and closer than Chena Hot Springs aurora tours, and the lodge stays open until ~3am with hot drinks.

attractions.name: Chatanika Lodge
attractions.season: Open year-round but with limited days - typically Thu-Sun; call ahead
attractions.blurb: 1970s roadhouse at Steese MP 28 crammed with mining junk and taxidermy; burgers, bar and 10 basic rooms.
attractions.tip: Popular winter aurora base at 28 mi out. Hours are short and it is closed several weekdays - phone before driving.

attractions.name: Chatanika Gold Camp
attractions.season: Viewable from the road year-round; buildings are private property
attractions.cost: Free to view from the road
attractions.blurb: Thirteen 1925 Fairbanks Exploration Company bunkhouses and shops on the NRHP, with dredge tailings all around.
attractions.tip: Privately owned and not a museum - look from the highway; do not walk into the buildings.

attractions.name: Poker Flat Research Range
attractions.season: Rocket campaigns run in winter aurora season; summer open-house tours some Saturdays
attractions.blurb: UAF Geophysical Institute rocket range at Steese MP 30 - the only university-owned launch site in the world.
attractions.tip: Sounding rockets fire into the aurora on winter nights; call the GI to ask about launch windows and summer tours.

attractions.name: Davidson Ditch
attractions.season: Best seen when the Steese is bare, roughly May-September
attractions.blurb: Remains of an 83-mi 1925 water aqueduct built to feed the Fairbanks gold dredges; a huge steel siphon crosses the road.
attractions.tip: The photogenic siphon and interpretive panels are around Steese MP 57 near the U.S. Creek Road turnoff.

attractions.name: Nome Creek Valley gold panning
attractions.season: Road open roughly June-September; U.S. Creek Road is steep gravel
attractions.blurb: BLM-designated 4-mi stretch of Nome Creek where anyone may pan for gold with hand tools, no claim or permit needed.
attractions.tip: Turn at Steese MP 57.3 onto U.S. Creek Road, 7 mi to Nome Creek Road; Ophir Creek and Mt. Prindle bracket the panning reach.

attractions.name: Twelvemile Summit
attractions.season: Snow-free roughly June-September; Fortymile caribou often cross late July-mid September
attractions.blurb: 3,190-ft alpine pass at Steese MP 85.5 with a viewing platform, on the Tanana-Yukon divide.
attractions.tip: One of the best road-accessible spots to catch the Fortymile caribou herd moving through - bring binoculars.

attractions.name: Eagle Summit
attractions.season: Snow-free roughly late June-September; drifts close it in winter storms
attractions.blurb: 3,624-ft treeless pass at Steese MP 107, the highest point on the road and a carpet of alpine wildflowers in July.
attractions.tip: On June 20-22 you can watch the sun stay above the horizon all night here - it's the classic Fairbanks solstice drive.

attractions.name: Pinnell Mountain National Recreation Trail
attractions.season: Snow-free roughly late June to mid-September; exposed ridgeline the whole way
attractions.blurb: 27.3-mi ridge traverse between Eagle Summit and Twelvemile Summit, entirely above treeline with two trail shelters.
attractions.tip: No water on the ridge - carry it or plan to drop to snow patches. Two-car shuttle or 3-day out-and-back.

attractions.name: Central & Circle District Museum
attractions.season: Museum open roughly Memorial Day to Labor Day, afternoons; town services year-round
attractions.blurb: Volunteer-run museum at Steese MP 127.7 covering the Circle Mining District, plus the last fuel before Circle.
attractions.tip: Central is the fuel/food stop for the upper Steese - Crabb's Corner has the only cafe and it keeps short hours.

attractions.name: Circle Hot Springs
attractions.season: Resort closed since 2002; the property is private and there is no public soaking
attractions.blurb: Abandoned 1930s hot-springs resort 8 mi off the Steese at Central; the hotel and pool sit empty and posted.
attractions.tip: Do not plan a soak here - it has been shut for over 20 years. Chena Hot Springs is the working alternative.

attractions.name: Circle
attractions.season: Road maintained year-round; boat access to the Yukon roughly June-September
attractions.blurb: End of the Steese Highway on the bank of the Yukon River, 50 mi south of the Arctic Circle despite the name.
attractions.tip: The boat launch is the downstream take-out for Yukon-Charley floats; gas and a small store, nothing else.

attractions.name: Elliott Highway
attractions.season: Open year-round; paved to the Dalton junction, gravel for the last 80 mi to Manley
attractions.blurb: 152-mi road from Fox past Livengood and the Dalton junction to Manley Hot Springs, with long ridge-top views.
attractions.tip: No services at Livengood (working camp, closed to visitors). Last fuel is the Hilltop truck stop at Fox.

attractions.name: Wickersham Dome Trailhead
attractions.season: Summer hiking roughly June-September; the same trailhead serves a big winter cabin-trail system
attractions.blurb: Paved-road trailhead at Elliott MP 28 into White Mountains NRA; a 5-mi climb above treeline toward Wickersham Dome.
attractions.tip: In winter this is the gateway to 200+ mi of ski/snowmachine trails and 12 reservable BLM cabins - book on recreation.gov.

attractions.name: Manley Hot Springs
attractions.season: Bathhouse open year-round by arrangement; roadhouse May-October
attractions.blurb: End-of-road village where 136F spring water heats a private greenhouse holding three concrete soaking tubs.
attractions.tip: You soak among grapevines inside the greenhouse and must phone the owners first (907-672-3231) to book a slot.

attractions.name: Minto
attractions.season: Road open year-round; Minto Road is 11 mi of gravel off Elliott MP 110
attractions.blurb: Tanana Athabascan village of ~150 relocated here in 1971; known for birch-bark baskets and Minto Flats.
attractions.tip: It is a living community, not an attraction - ask before photographing, and buy crafts directly from makers.

attractions.name: Gold Dredge 8
attractions.season: 2026 tour season roughly May 11-Sept 19, departures 10:30am and 1:45pm
attractions.blurb: 1928 five-deck bucket-line dredge on the NRHP; narrow-gauge train ride, permafrost cut and guaranteed gold panning.
attractions.tip: Your panning poke gets weighed and can be set into jewellery on site - factor in the upsell. Book online in July.

attractions.name: El Dorado Gold Mine
attractions.season: Historically ran May-mid September; status uncertain - its website now redirects to Gold Dredge 8
attractions.blurb: Family placer operation at Elliott Hwy MP 1.3 with a replica Tanana Valley Railroad car and a permafrost tunnel.
attractions.tip: Confirm it is operating before driving out - eldoradogoldmine.com now forwards to Gold Dredge 8's booking site.

attractions.name: Tok
attractions.season: Year-round crossroads; most visitor services May-September, a few open all winter
attractions.blurb: Highway junction town where the Alaska Highway meets the Tok Cutoff - the first real services after the border.
attractions.tip: Everyone driving the Alaska Highway funnels through here; it is the last full-service stop before Chicken or Delta.

attractions.name: Tok Alaska Public Lands Information Center
attractions.season: Open daily in summer, roughly mid-May to mid-September; reduced or closed in winter
attractions.blurb: Interagency center at Alaska Hwy MP 1314 with wildlife mounts, statewide trip-planning help and free maps.
attractions.tip: Best place to get current Taylor Highway and Denali road conditions before you commit to the drive north.

attractions.name: Mukluk Land
attractions.season: Open daily 2-7pm, June 1 to August 20 only
attractions.blurb: Junkyard theme park 3 mi west of Tok: giant mosquito, doll museum, outhouse collection, mini-golf and skee-ball.
attractions.tip: Only open five hours a day for 11 weeks - plan your Tok arrival for the afternoon if you want in.

attractions.name: Tetlin National Wildlife Refuge Visitor Center
attractions.season: Center staffed roughly mid-May to mid-September; refuge lands open year-round
attractions.blurb: Sod-roofed log center at Alaska Hwy MP 1229 with spotting scopes on a bluff over the Tetlin lakes and wetlands.
attractions.tip: First US stop after the border - great early-morning waterfowl and swan viewing; sandhill cranes stage here in May.

attractions.name: Deadman Lake Campground
attractions.season: Open roughly mid-May to mid-September
attractions.blurb: Free 15-site refuge campground at Alaska Hwy MP 1249 with a boat ramp, photo blind and quarter-mile Taiga Trail.
attractions.tip: Free camping with a wildlife-viewing deck; expect ferocious mosquitoes in June - bring a head net.

attractions.name: Alaska-Canada Border at Port Alcan
attractions.season: US port open 24 hrs year-round; Canadian side at Beaver Creek also 24 hrs
attractions.blurb: The Alaska Highway's international crossing at Alaska Hwy MP 1221.8, on the 141st meridian survey swath.
attractions.tip: Set your clock: Alaska is 1 hr behind Yukon. Cleared 20-ft boundary vista runs arrow-straight to the horizon.

attractions.name: Northway
attractions.season: Village road open year-round; airport was the historic Lend-Lease and customs stop
attractions.blurb: Upper Tanana Athabascan village 7 mi off the Alaska Highway, built around a WWII Lend-Lease route airfield.
attractions.tip: Northway Airport is still a port-of-entry airstrip for small planes; the village itself has minimal services.

attractions.name: Delta Junction - End of the Alaska Highway
attractions.season: Visitor center open roughly mid-May to mid-September; town year-round
attractions.blurb: Official end of the 1,422-mi Alaska Highway, marked by a milepost monument at the Richardson Highway junction.
attractions.tip: The Sullivan Roadhouse museum, a relocated 1905 Valdez-Fairbanks trail roadhouse, sits beside the visitor center.

attractions.name: Big Delta State Historical Park & Rika's Roadhouse
attractions.season: Grounds open mid-May to mid-September; cafe and gift shop roughly May 15-Sept 5, 10am-4pm
attractions.blurb: Restored 1913 roadhouse and 10-acre park on the Tanana at the old Valdez-Fairbanks trail river crossing.
attractions.tip: The trans-Alaska pipeline's Tanana River suspension span is visible from the grounds - best photo of it anywhere.
